src/dimslash/sync

      • canonicalize(commands: JsonNode): JsonNode

      • resolveApplicationId(handler: InteractionHandler): Future[string]
        syncCommands
      • syncCommands(handler: InteractionHandler; force = false): Future[seq[SyncResult]]
        toCommandJson
      • toCommandJson(cmd: MessageCommand): JsonNode
      • toCommandJson(cmd: SlashCommand): JsonNode
      • toCommandJson(cmd: UserCommand): JsonNode
        toOptionJson
      • toOptionJson(spec: OptionSpec): JsonNode

Command synchronization with diffing.

syncCommands groups registered commands by scope (global + each guild), fetches what Discord currently has, and only PUTs a scope when the payload actually differs — so restarting a bot no longer rewrites every command on every boot.

dimslash builds the JSON itself (toCommandJson): dimscord 1.8.0's serializer silently drops min_value/max_value/min_length/ max_length/channel_types.

Comparison happens on a canonical form (canonicalize) that keeps a whitelist of writable fields, materializes Discord's defaults, and sorts commands — so server-added echo fields can never cause a spurious PUT.

Caveat: only scopes that currently contain at least one registered command are synced. If you remove the last command of a guild (or global), clear that scope manually or with an empty bulk overwrite.

Imports

types

Types

SyncResult = object
  scope*: string             ## "" = global, otherwise a guild id
  commandCount*: int
  updated*: bool             ## false = Discord already matched

Procs

proc canonicalize(commands: JsonNode): JsonNode {....raises: [KeyError],
    tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Reduces a command-array payload (ours or Discord's) to the comparable subset: writable fields only, defaults materialized, commands sorted by (type, name). 
proc resolveApplicationId(handler: InteractionHandler): Future[string] {.
    ...stackTrace: false, raises: [Exception, ValueError], tags: [RootEffect],
    forbids: [].}
Explicitly set handler.applicationId beats asking Discord; the result is cached either way. 
proc syncCommands(handler: InteractionHandler; force = false): Future[
    seq[SyncResult]] {....stackTrace: false,
                       raises: [Exception, ValueError, KeyError],
                       tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Pushes registered commands to Discord, per scope, skipping scopes whose commands already match. force = true always overwrites. Call once after startup (e.g. in the onReady event). 
proc toCommandJson(cmd: MessageCommand): JsonNode {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}

    forbids: [].}
proc toCommandJson(cmd: UserCommand): JsonNode {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
proc toOptionJson(spec: OptionSpec): JsonNode {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
